title: Distributed SSH Brute-Force
briefing: The SSH service on $TARGET is being hit with failed logins from several different external addresses at once — a distributed guessing attempt rather than a single noisy host. Enumerate every source, confirm the target, classify the activity and recommend containment.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 5710. Group by data.srcip — expect MORE THAN TWO distinct sources. Make sure you list them all.

questions.explain: Four external IPs ($SRC1, $SRC2, $SRC3, $SRC4) each generated SSH auth failures against the same host — miss any and you under-scope the block list.

questions.prompt: What class of attack is this?
questions.explain: Many external sources, repeated failed authentication = distributed credential brute force.

options: Block all the source IPs (or the ranges) at the firewall
Just block the first IP you saw
answer: Block all the source IPs (or the ranges) at the firewall
questions.explain: Block ALL sources, rate-limit, key-only auth. Blocking one IP leaves the rest; ignoring is wrong.
